sections.paragraph:
ϑρόνος, ου, ὁ [akin to ϑρᾶνος ‘bench’; ‘chair, seat’] throne (the customary gloss reflecting the status of the one for whom a chair is set aside)

—a. of human seat of power Lk 1:32, 52; Ac 2:30.

—b. of God’s seat of power Hb 1:8; 12:2; Rv 1:4; 7:15. Of heaven as God’s throne Mt 5:34; 23:22; Ac 7:49.

—c. by metonymy in pl. as collective the enthroned Col 1:16.
